hypothesis: When the GraphSAGE framework is extended to three-or-more-component gas mixtures (e.g., Ar/O₂/CF₄), using higher-order intermediate layer representations from pre-trained single-element networks as node features will yield disproportionately greater accuracy gains relative to the two-component case, because the combinatorial interaction complexity grows super-linearly with the number of mixture components, making the richer chemistry-specific information encoded in hidden layers increasingly critical for the graph aggregation mechanism to correctly model multi-body gas-phase and surface interaction effects.
